zotero.abstractNote: As large language models (LLMs) are increasingly deployed globally, it is critical to understand whether their moral judgments align with human values across different cultures. We introduce a multilingual trolley problem dataset, covering 12 languages and 14 cultures, to evaluate the moral alignment of LLMs. We find that while LLMs show a high degree of moral consistency across languages, they also exhibit significant cultural biases. Our results suggest that current alignment techniques may not be sufficient to ensure cross-cultural moral alignment, and we propose a framework for future research in this direction.
